family_label: How it relates to the parent
definition: This move has a challenge.
boolean_question: Does this move have at least one child that is structurally a challenge / rebuttal?
yes_meaning: An auto-metadata fact derived from the argument tree: at least one of the move's children is typed as a challenge. Deterministic from the tree.
no_meaning: The move has no children typed as challenges (it may have other children like clarifications, supports, or branches).
mapped_messages: Challenged; Has a challenge; This move has a challenge.

family_label: How it relates to the parent
definition: This move has a counter-challenge.
boolean_question: Does this move have at least one grandchild that is structurally a counter-rebuttal (challenge to its child challenge)?
yes_meaning: An auto-metadata fact derived from the argument tree: the move has a child challenge that itself has a challenge child. Deterministic.
no_meaning: No counter-rebuttal descends from this move (the challenge has no counter-challenge child).
mapped_messages: Counter; Has a counter-challenge; This move has a counter-challenge.

family_label: How it relates to the parent
definition: This cluster is under pressure from a challenge.
boolean_question: Is this cluster in the "rebutted" lifecycle state — an open challenge has been posted and not yet answered?
yes_meaning: A lifecycle state derived from the cluster's response pattern: at least one challenge to the cluster's main claim is open (not closed by a clarification / sourcing / concession / synthesis).
no_meaning: The cluster has no open challenge, or every challenge has been answered. The cluster moved to a different lifecycle state.
mapped_messages: Pressured; Under pressure; This cluster is under pressure from a challenge.

family_label: How it relates to the parent
definition: This reply is anchored to the parent by a quote.
boolean_question: Does this move quote specific text from its parent and use that quote as the anchor for its response?
yes_meaning: The move contains a direct quotation of a portion of the parent's text and structures its response around that quote — addressing the quoted portion specifically rather than the parent's general thrust.
no_meaning: The move paraphrases the parent without quoting, or references the parent without a quote, or quotes something that is not the parent (an external source). A passing quote without anchoring (the move's substantive response is elsewhere) is NOT this rawKey.
mapped_messages: Anchored reply; Anchored; This reply is anchored to the parent by a quote.

family_label: How it relates to the parent
definition: This move provides support for its parent argument.
boolean_question: Does this move's substantive content support (rather than challenge, refine, or be neutral about) its parent's position?
yes_meaning: The move advances reasons, evidence, or examples that strengthen the parent's claim or position. The move may add new support, restate the parent's claim from a different angle, or provide a confirming example.
no_meaning: The move challenges, qualifies, redirects, or is independent of the parent's position. A move that says 'yes, and ALSO...' for a DIFFERENT axis is NOT support — that is extends_parent.
mapped_messages: Supports parent; Supports; This move provides support for its parent argument.

family_label: How it relates to the parent
definition: This move challenges its parent argument.
boolean_question: Does this move's substantive content challenge (dispute, push back on, raise problems with) its parent's claim, scope, evidence, or reasoning?
yes_meaning: The move's substantive content pushes back on the parent in at least one of: factual challenge, scope challenge, evidence-applicability challenge, definition dispute, causal disagreement, value disagreement.
no_meaning: The move agrees, refines on the same side, questions for clarification, summarizes, or acknowledges. Pure questions and pure paraphrases are NOT challenges.
mapped_messages: Challenges parent; Challenges; This move challenges its parent argument.

family_label: How it relates to the parent
definition: This move refines or narrows the parent's claim.
boolean_question: Does this move take the parent's claim and offer a refinement that narrows or sharpens it (same side, more precise)?
yes_meaning: The move accepts the parent's broad direction and proposes a more precise / narrower / better-scoped version. Move-intrinsic; distinct from lifecycle 'narrowed' (which is cluster-state after acceptance).
no_meaning: The move challenges or disputes, extends to a different axis, or merely acknowledges. A refinement is supportive narrowing.
mapped_messages: Refines parent; Refines; This move refines or narrows the parent's claim.

family_label: How it relates to the parent
definition: This move extends the parent to a related but distinct point.
boolean_question: Does this move accept the parent's point and extend it to a related but distinct point on the same side?
yes_meaning: The move signals 'yes, AND also...' — accepting the parent and adding a related but distinct same-side point. The extension is not the same claim restated; it is an additional claim coalition-building with the parent.
no_meaning: The move challenges, refines (narrows the same claim), or extends to a DIFFERENT axis. Pure agreement without extension is acknowledges_parent.
mapped_messages: Extends parent; Extends; This move extends the parent to a related but distinct point.

family_label: How it relates to the parent
definition: This move draws a sub-distinction the parent did not.
boolean_question: Does this move draw a sub-distinction within the parent's scope that the parent treated as unified?
yes_meaning: The move splits a category the parent treated as one ('libraries' → 'urban libraries vs rural libraries'; 'EVs' → 'passenger EVs vs commercial EVs'). The distinction matters for the parent's claim.
no_meaning: The move accepts the parent's scope without subdividing, or proposes an entirely new axis (introduces_new_issue), or challenges the whole claim.
mapped_messages: Distinguishes from parent; Distinguishes; This move draws a sub-distinction the parent did not.

family_label: How it relates to the parent
definition: This move offers a different frame for the parent's topic.
boolean_question: Does this move re-frame the topic, shifting the lens or the decision criterion the parent assumed?
yes_meaning: The move accepts the topic but proposes a different frame: 'this is really a question of fairness, not efficiency'; 'the right unit is per-resident, not per-county'; 'we should be asking who pays, not whether to pay'. Reframing changes the criterion.
no_meaning: The move accepts the frame and challenges the claim within it, or extends, or refines, or introduces a new issue without reframing.
mapped_messages: Reframes parent; Reframes; This move offers a different frame for the parent's topic.

family_label: How it relates to the parent
definition: This move asks a substantive question about the parent.
boolean_question: Does this move ask a substantive question about the parent's claim, evidence, or reasoning (not just a clarification)?
yes_meaning: The move poses a real question that probes the parent's basis: 'how do you square that with X?'; 'what would falsify this?'; 'have you considered Y?'. The question carries genuine engagement, not just clarification.
no_meaning: The move is a pure clarification request (requests_clarification), a challenge framed as a question (challenges_parent), or rhetorical question with no engagement.
mapped_messages: Questions parent; Questions; This move asks a substantive question about the parent.

family_label: How it relates to the parent
definition: This move summarizes the parent's position.
boolean_question: Does this move summarize the parent's position in its own words?
yes_meaning: The move restates the parent's position in compressed form, preserving the parent's intent. May be in service of a follow-up move (challenge, support, refine) or stand on its own.
no_meaning: The move quotes the parent verbatim (quote_anchors_parent), paraphrases inaccurately, or proceeds without restating.
mapped_messages: Summarizes parent; Summary; This move summarizes the parent's position.

family_label: How it relates to the parent
definition: This move acknowledges the parent without disputing or adding.
boolean_question: Does this move express acknowledgement of the parent ('OK', 'I see', 'fair point') without adding substantive content?
yes_meaning: The move signals agreement, understanding, or willingness to engage, but adds no substantive support, refinement, challenge, or extension.
no_meaning: The move adds substantive content beyond acknowledgement (which would be supports_parent / extends_parent / refines_parent etc.).
mapped_messages: Acknowledges parent; Ack; This move acknowledges the parent without disputing or adding.

family_label: How it relates to the parent
definition: This move corrects a specific factual detail in the parent.
boolean_question: Does this move correct a specific factual detail in the parent (a number, name, date, attribution) without challenging the parent's overall claim?
yes_meaning: The move points out an error in a specific factual detail and offers the correct value, while leaving the parent's main claim intact. Narrower than challenges_parent.
no_meaning: The move challenges the parent's overall claim, or quibbles with detail without offering a correction, or asks for clarification about the detail.
mapped_messages: Corrects parent detail; Corrects; This move corrects a specific factual detail in the parent.

family_label: How it relates to the parent
definition: This move draws a contrast that highlights a tension with the parent.
boolean_question: Does this move draw a contrast between two cases / examples / domains to highlight a tension with the parent's claim?
yes_meaning: The move presents a comparison ('whereas X, Y') in service of showing the parent's claim does not hold uniformly. The contrast is the move's main vehicle.
no_meaning: The move offers a direct challenge, supports with a similar case, distinguishes within the parent's scope (distinguishes_parent), or extends.
mapped_messages: Contrasts with parent; Contrasts; This move draws a contrast that highlights a tension with the parent.

family_label: How it relates to the parent
definition: This move answers a question posed by the parent.
boolean_question: Does this move answer a question that was posed in the parent move?
yes_meaning: The parent contained a question (request for evidence, clarification, or substantive question). The move provides a direct response to that question.
no_meaning: The parent contained no question, or the move ignores the question and responds to something else, or the move is a counter-question without an answer.
mapped_messages: Answers parent question; Answers; This move answers a question posed by the parent.

definition: The applicability of evidence is challenged.
boolean_question: Does this move challenge the APPLICABILITY of the evidence the parent (or a prior move) cited — arguing that the evidence does not actually bear on the claim?
yes_meaning: The move accepts that the cited evidence exists and is correct, but argues that it does not apply to the claim at hand — wrong population, wrong time period, wrong setting, wrong measurement, or wrong inferential step.
no_meaning: The move challenges the evidence's factual accuracy (that is evidence_quality_questioned in Family D), the underlying claim directly (challenges_parent in Family A), or asks for evidence (asks_for_evidence in Family D).
mapped_messages: Evidence applicability challenged; App. dispute; The applicability of evidence is challenged.

definition: This move expresses disagreement with its parent.
boolean_question: Does this move express disagreement with any aspect of its parent (claim, scope, definition, causal link, evidence, value, etc.)?
yes_meaning: The move signals at least one disagreement axis with the parent: factual disagreement, scope challenge, definitional dispute, evidence-applicability challenge, causal disagreement, value disagreement, priority disagreement, or generalization challenge.
no_meaning: The move supports, refines (narrows on the same side), extends to a different axis, acknowledges, summarizes, or is purely a clarification request. Pure agreement, pure question-for-clarification, or pure same-side extension is NOT disagreement.
mapped_messages: Disagreement; Disagrees; This move expresses disagreement with its parent.

definition: This move disputes how a term is defined.
boolean_question: Does this move dispute the parent's definition of a key term — arguing the term should be defined differently?
yes_meaning: The move targets a definition the parent assumed: 'when you say infrastructure, that includes/excludes X'; 'efficiency means Y, not Z'; 'the right unit is per-resident, not per-county'. Definitional disagreement is the move's main thrust.
no_meaning: The move disputes facts within an accepted definition, asks for definitional clarification (Family C requests_clarification), or proposes a shared definition (Family C proposes_shared_definition — collaborative, not adversarial).
mapped_messages: Disputes definition; Def. dispute; This move disputes how a term is defined.

definition: This move disputes the scope of the parent's claim.
boolean_question: Does this move dispute the SCOPE of the parent's claim — arguing the claim applies only to a narrower or different population, setting, or time window?
yes_meaning: The move argues the parent's claim does not generalize to the full scope the parent implied. The challenge identifies a specific exclusion: 'true for X but not Y'; 'works in the short term but not long term'; 'applies in dense cities but not rural'.
no_meaning: The move challenges the claim within its accepted scope (challenges_parent), refines scope collaboratively (refines_parent), or asks about scope (questions_parent).
mapped_messages: Disputes scope; Scope dispute; This move disputes the scope of the parent's claim.

definition: This move disputes a specific factual claim.
boolean_question: Does this move dispute a specific factual claim in the parent (a number, an event, an attribution, a measurement)?
yes_meaning: The move identifies a specific factual claim in the parent and offers a counter-claim ('the figure is X, not Y'; 'the event happened in 2019, not 2020'; 'that was the Jones paper, not Smith').
no_meaning: The move offers a correction of a non-disputed detail (corrects_parent_detail in Family A — supportive correction), disputes scope/applicability/definition rather than fact, or asks for evidence.
mapped_messages: Disputes fact; Fact dispute; This move disputes a specific factual claim.

definition: This move disputes a causal claim in the parent.
boolean_question: Does this move dispute that the parent's claimed cause actually causes the claimed effect (or that the direction of causation is right)?
yes_meaning: The move argues against the causal link: 'X does not cause Y'; 'correlation is not causation here'; 'the causation runs the other way'; 'the real cause is Z, not X'.
no_meaning: The move accepts the causation and disputes the magnitude (disputes_fact), the applicability (disputes_evidence_applicability), or scope (disputes_scope). The move questions causation without asserting an alternative (more like questions_parent).
mapped_messages: Disputes causal link; Causal dispute; This move disputes a causal claim in the parent.

definition: This move disputes how the parent weights competing values.
boolean_question: Does this move dispute how the parent weights competing values — arguing a different value should take priority?
yes_meaning: The move accepts the empirical landscape and disputes how the parent weights values: 'efficiency matters but equity matters more here'; 'security and privacy are both real, but privacy should win'. The disagreement is normative.
no_meaning: The move disputes facts, scope, definition, or evidence — not values. The move proposes a synthesis of values (Family G synthesis_proposed) without disputing the parent's weighting.
mapped_messages: Disputes value weighting; Value dispute; This move disputes how the parent weights competing values.

definition: This move disputes the criterion being used to evaluate the decision.
boolean_question: Does this move dispute the CRITERION the parent uses to evaluate the decision — arguing a different criterion should govern?
yes_meaning: The parent applies a decision criterion (cost, effectiveness, fairness, feasibility, etc.). The move argues a different criterion should govern: 'cost is not the right test here — feasibility is'; 'effectiveness without equity is the wrong frame'.
no_meaning: The move accepts the criterion and disputes facts or weight within it. The move proposes a synthesis of criteria. Pure value disagreement (disputes_value_weighting) is closely related but criterion is the evaluation procedure, value is the underlying good.
mapped_messages: Disputes decision criterion; Crit. dispute; This move disputes the criterion being used to evaluate the decision.

definition: This move disputes a generalization in the parent.
boolean_question: Does this move dispute a generalization the parent made — arguing from a specific case to challenge a universal claim?
yes_meaning: The move offers one or more counter-examples to a parent generalization: 'you say X is always true; here is a case where X is false'. Often takes the form 'all/most X are Y → but here are X that are not Y'.
no_meaning: The move disputes scope (which is the universal/particular dimension) rather than a generalization per se. The move offers a counter-example as a refinement (refines_parent) rather than as a generalization-challenge.
mapped_messages: Disputes generalization; Gen. dispute; This move disputes a generalization in the parent.

definition: This move disputes an analogy the parent used.
boolean_question: Does this move dispute an analogy the parent used — arguing that the source case does not map to the target case?
yes_meaning: The parent used an analogy (X is like Y; from Y we infer Z about X). The move challenges the mapping: 'X is not like Y in the relevant respect'; 'Y\'s lesson does not transfer because of difference D'.
no_meaning: The move offers a counter-analogy (introduces_new_issue with an analogy frame), accepts the analogy and challenges its inference, or extends the analogy (extends_parent).
mapped_messages: Disputes analogy; Analogy disp.; This move disputes an analogy the parent used.

definition: This move disputes the parent's interpretation of evidence or text.
boolean_question: Does this move dispute the parent's interpretation of a shared piece of evidence, text, or event — arguing for a different reading?
yes_meaning: Both sides share a piece of evidence / text / event. The move argues the parent's interpretation is one reading but not the only one; offers an alternate reading and reasons for it.
no_meaning: The move offers a new piece of evidence (provides_evidence, Family D), questions the applicability (disputes_evidence_applicability), or challenges the underlying fact (disputes_fact). The move offers an alternate interpretation collaboratively (Family C provides_alternate_interpretation).
mapped_messages: Disputes interpretation; Interp. disp.; This move disputes the parent's interpretation of evidence or text.

definition: This move disputes the order in which the parent prioritizes options.
boolean_question: Does this move dispute the priority order the parent assigned to options — arguing for a different sequencing?
yes_meaning: The parent ordered options (do X before Y, address A then B). The move argues for a different priority: 'Y before X'; 'address B first because of dependency D'.
no_meaning: The move disputes that one of the options should be pursued at all (challenges_parent), disputes the criterion used to set priorities (disputes_decision_criterion), or proposes adding/removing an option from the list.
mapped_messages: Disputes priority order; Prio dispute; This move disputes the order in which the parent prioritizes options.

definition: This move disputes the proposed remedy or solution.
boolean_question: Does this move accept the problem framing but dispute the proposed remedy / solution — arguing for a different fix?
yes_meaning: Parent diagnoses a problem and proposes a remedy. The move accepts the diagnosis but argues the proposed remedy will not work, has side effects, or is inferior to an alternative remedy.
no_meaning: The move disputes the diagnosis itself (challenges_parent), accepts the remedy with refinements (refines_parent), or proposes an additive remedy (extends_parent).
mapped_messages: Disputes remedy; Remedy disp.; This move disputes the proposed remedy or solution.

definition: This move disputes that the parent's point is relevant.
boolean_question: Does this move dispute that the parent's point is RELEVANT to the topic under discussion?
yes_meaning: The move accepts the parent's point may be true but argues it does not bear on the current question. 'That is interesting but tangential'; 'true but unrelated to whether X should happen'.
no_meaning: The move accepts relevance and disputes facts/scope/etc. The move says the topic itself should change (introduces_new_issue / reframes_parent). Pure tangent flag without engagement is just dismissal.
mapped_messages: Disputes relevance; Relev. disp.; This move disputes that the parent's point is relevant.

definition: A clarification was provided in this cluster.
boolean_question: Is this cluster in the "clarified" lifecycle state — a clarification request was answered with a clarifying response?
yes_meaning: A lifecycle state where the cluster contains a clarification request and at least one clarifying response (answers_clarification or equivalent repair move).
no_meaning: The cluster has no clarification request, or the request is open with no answer, or the request was answered with a non-clarifying move.
mapped_messages: Clarified; A clarification was provided in this cluster.

definition: A clarification is requested here.
boolean_question: Does this move ask the parent's poster to clarify a term, claim, scope, or reasoning step?
yes_meaning: The move poses a question seeking a restatement, definition, scope, or elaboration. 'What do you mean by X?'; 'how broad is this claim?'; 'do you mean the 2020 or 2024 data?'. Pure clarification — no challenge attached.
no_meaning: The move offers a candidate understanding (offers_candidate_understanding), challenges packaged as a question (challenges_parent), or asks for evidence (asks_for_evidence, Family D).
mapped_messages: Clarification asked; Clarify?; A clarification is requested here.

definition: A clarification was answered here.
boolean_question: Does this move provide a substantive answer to a clarification request from the parent?
yes_meaning: The move responds to a clarification request with a substantive answer: a definition, a scope, an elaboration, a restatement. Closes the clarification cycle.
no_meaning: The move evades the question, offers a counter-question, or responds with new content unrelated to the clarification.
mapped_messages: Clarification answered; Clarified; A clarification was answered here.

definition: An alternate interpretation is offered.
boolean_question: Does this move offer an alternate interpretation of the parent (or a prior move's) text or evidence, framed collaboratively rather than adversarially?
yes_meaning: The move offers a different reading of the parent's text / evidence, inviting consideration rather than disputing. 'Could it also mean Y?'; 'another reading is Z; what do you think?'. Collaborative.
no_meaning: The move disputes the interpretation directly (disputes_interpretation, Family B), offers a counter-claim without inviting consideration, or asks a pure clarification.
mapped_messages: Alternate reading; Alt read; An alternate interpretation is offered.

definition: This move offers a paraphrase of the parent's claim for confirmation.
boolean_question: Does this move offer a paraphrase of the parent's claim and ask the parent's poster to confirm or correct that paraphrase?
yes_meaning: The move restates the parent's claim in its own words (a 'candidate understanding') AND signals openness to correction. The move signals 'do you mean X?' explicitly OR via paraphrase + invitation to correct.
no_meaning: The move quotes the parent verbatim without paraphrasing, OR paraphrases without inviting correction, OR asks for clarification without offering a candidate. Pure question ('what do you mean?') is requests_clarification, not this.
mapped_messages: Offers candidate understanding; "Do you mean..."; This move offers a paraphrase of the parent's claim for confirmation.

definition: This move confirms a candidate understanding the parent offered.
boolean_question: Does this move confirm a candidate understanding that the parent offered, sealing the grounding cycle?
yes_meaning: The parent offered a candidate understanding ('do you mean X?'). The move confirms: 'yes, that is right'; 'exactly'; 'you have it'. Closes the grounding cycle on the affirmative side.
no_meaning: The parent offered no candidate understanding (so there is nothing to confirm), or the move corrects rather than confirms (rejects_candidate_understanding), or the move adds substantive new content.
mapped_messages: Confirms candidate; Confirms; This move confirms a candidate understanding the parent offered.

definition: This move corrects the candidate understanding the parent offered.
boolean_question: Does this move reject the candidate understanding offered by the parent and provide a corrected version?
yes_meaning: Parent offered candidate understanding ('do you mean X?'); the move rejects X and offers the correct paraphrase: 'no, I mean Y'. Symmetric counterpart to confirms_understanding.
no_meaning: Parent offered no candidate understanding, the move confirms (confirms_understanding), or the move challenges substantively rather than correcting the paraphrase.
mapped_messages: Corrects candidate; Corrects; This move corrects the candidate understanding the parent offered.

definition: This move asks the parent to restate their position.
boolean_question: Does this move ask the parent's poster to restate their position (without asking for new content)?
yes_meaning: The move signals 'can you say that again?' / 'I lost the thread' / 'can you rephrase' — a request for restatement without substantive challenge or candidate understanding.
no_meaning: The move asks for clarification (different — clarification is asking for definitional / scope precision; restatement is asking for rephrasing). The move offers a candidate understanding instead.
mapped_messages: Asks for restatement; Restate?; This move asks the parent to restate their position.

definition: The author self-initiates a correction or clarification of their own prior move.
boolean_question: Does this move's author self-correct or self-clarify their own prior move, without being prompted to?
yes_meaning: The author posts a follow-up that corrects, clarifies, or refines their own earlier move. Schegloff/Sacks 'self-initiated self-repair'.
no_meaning: The author is responding to a prompt (request for clarification / challenge / correction) from another participant — that is other-initiated repair (other_initiates_repair) or answers_clarification.
mapped_messages: Self-repair; The author self-initiates a correction or clarification of their own prior move.

definition: This move initiates repair of a prior move by a different participant.
boolean_question: Does this move initiate repair on a prior move from a different participant (asking for clarification, correction, or restatement)?
yes_meaning: A participant other than the parent's author initiates repair: requests_clarification, requests_restatement, offers_candidate_understanding, flags_term_ambiguity, etc. Schegloff/Sacks 'other-initiated repair'.
no_meaning: Self-initiated repair (self_initiates_self_repair) is the symmetric counterpart. Substantive disagreement is not repair (challenges_parent).
mapped_messages: Other-initiated repair; Repair?; This move initiates repair of a prior move by a different participant.

definition: This move acknowledges that the author misread the parent.
boolean_question: Does this move acknowledge that the author misread or misunderstood the parent (or a prior move), and offer a corrected reading?
yes_meaning: The move signals 'oh, I misread that' / 'I had you wrong' / 'sorry, I thought you meant X' and provides a corrected reading or revised response.
no_meaning: The move continues responding to a misread without acknowledging, or challenges the parent on substance, or offers a candidate understanding without acknowledging prior misreading.

definition: This move flags an ambiguous reference in the parent.
boolean_question: Does this move flag an ambiguous reference (pronoun, demonstrative, or referring expression) in the parent and ask which referent was meant?
yes_meaning: The parent contained an ambiguous reference ('this', 'they', 'it' with unclear antecedent). The move identifies the ambiguity and asks which referent the parent meant.
no_meaning: The move asks for general clarification (requests_clarification), challenges the substance, or refers to a clearly-grounded antecedent.

definition: This move flags an ambiguous term in the parent.

yes_meaning: The move identifies an ambiguous term ('infrastructure', 'efficiency', 'works') and notes the term could mean A, B, or C, then asks which the parent meant. More specific than requests_clarification.
no_meaning: The move asks for general clarification, requests a definition without naming the ambiguity, or disputes the term's definition (disputes_definition).

yes_meaning: The move offers a specific definition with the framing 'could we use this definition?' or 'let me propose X means Y for purposes of this discussion'. Collaborative grounding move.

yes_meaning: Lifecycle state: the cluster had a source request, and at least one source has been attached.
no_meaning: No source request, OR source request is open with no attachment.
mapped_messages: Sourced; Source attached; A source has been attached in this cluster.

definition: A direct quote has been requested.
boolean_question: Is this cluster in the quote_requested lifecycle state — a quote request is open?
yes_meaning: Lifecycle state: an open quote request exists in the cluster.
no_meaning: No quote request, or quote request is closed by attachment.
mapped_messages: Quote asked; Quote requested; A direct quote has been requested.

definition: A source has been requested.
boolean_question: Is this cluster in the source_requested lifecycle state — a source request is open?
yes_meaning: Lifecycle state: an open source request exists in the cluster.
no_meaning: No source request, or source request is closed by attachment.
mapped_messages: Source asked; Source requested; A source has been requested.

definition: Evidence is requested here.
boolean_question: Does this move ask the parent (or a prior move) to produce evidence for a claim?
yes_meaning: The move contains an explicit evidence request: "what is your source?"; "where does that number come from?"; "can you cite something?".
no_meaning: The move asks for clarification of meaning (requests_clarification, Family C), challenges substantively (challenges_parent), or provides evidence itself.
mapped_messages: Evidence requested; Evidence?; Evidence is requested here.

definition: Evidence is provided here.
boolean_question: Does this move provide evidence (source, citation, quote, data) for a claim?
yes_meaning: The move attaches or references substantive evidence: a citation, a source URL, a quote, a data point with attribution.
no_meaning: The move asserts a claim without evidence (evidence_gap_present), or makes a value claim that does not require evidence, or asks for evidence.
mapped_messages: Evidence provided; Evidence; Evidence is provided here.

definition: Evidence appears to match the claim.
boolean_question: Does the evidence attached to this move actually support the specific claim being made?
yes_meaning: The evidence (source, quote, data) attached is relevant to the claim and is being used in a way consistent with what the source says.
no_meaning: The evidence does not match the claim (wrong scope, wrong population, cherry-picked, mischaracterized), OR no evidence is attached.
mapped_messages: Evidence matched to claim; Matched; Evidence appears to match the claim.

definition: A gap in the source chain has appeared.
boolean_question: Does this move introduce a source-chain gap — a claim that depends on a source that is not provided or whose chain to the primary source is broken?
yes_meaning: The move makes a claim citing a secondary or tertiary source without linking back to the primary source (or fails to cite at all when the source is required).
no_meaning: The move cites primary sources cleanly, OR repairs a prior gap (source_chain_repair), OR makes a claim that does not require sourcing.
mapped_messages: Source gap; A gap in the source chain has appeared.

definition: An evidence debt was opened.
boolean_question: Does this move open an evidence debt — a claim that has not yet been backed by evidence and is now visible as a pending obligation?
yes_meaning: The move makes a claim that requires evidence but provides none, AND the system marks the claim as creating an evidence-debt ledger entry.
yes_message: Good pressure — an evidence debt opened.
no_meaning: The move provides evidence, closes prior debt, or makes a non-evidence-requiring claim.
mapped_messages: Evidence debt opened; Debt open; Good pressure — an evidence debt opened.; An evidence debt was opened.

definition: An evidence debt was answered.
boolean_question: Does this move close a prior open evidence debt by providing the required evidence?
yes_meaning: The move attaches evidence that closes an outstanding evidence-debt ledger entry from a prior move.
no_meaning: The move opens new debt, provides evidence for a current claim without a prior open debt, or does not engage with evidence.
mapped_messages: Evidence debt answered; Debt closed; An evidence debt was answered.

definition: A corroborating document was supplied.
boolean_question: Does this move supply a corroborating document — additional independent evidence supporting a claim that already had some evidence?
yes_meaning: The move supplies a SECOND or LATER piece of evidence for a claim, from an INDEPENDENT source.
no_meaning: The move provides the first evidence (provides_evidence), supplies an additional source from the same provider, or restates existing evidence.
mapped_messages: Corroborating document; Corroborated; A corroborating document was supplied.

definition: This move provides a source.
boolean_question: Does this move provide a source for a claim, either its own claim or one in the parent / prior move?
yes_meaning: The move names a source (citation / URL / DOI / institution) in service of a claim. Move-intrinsic act distinct from source_attached (auto-metadata fact).
no_meaning: The move asks for a source (asks_for_evidence), mentions a source without using it as backing, or makes a claim without sourcing.
mapped_messages: Source provided; Source +; This move provides a source.

definition: This move provides a direct quote.
boolean_question: Does this move provide a direct quote from a source in service of a claim?
yes_meaning: The move includes verbatim quoted text from a source (paper, statute, article, transcript) and uses it as backing for a claim.
no_meaning: The move paraphrases without quoting, asks for a quote (asks_for_evidence in quote form), or quotes the parent (quote_anchors_parent in Family A).
mapped_messages: Quote provided; Quote +; This move provides a direct quote.

definition: A concrete example is requested.
boolean_question: Does this move ask the parent (or prior move) to provide a concrete example for an abstract claim?
yes_meaning: The move requests a concrete example: "what is an example of that?"; "can you point to a specific case?"; "have you seen this work somewhere?".
no_meaning: The move asks for evidence in general (asks_for_evidence), asks for clarification (requests_clarification), or makes a substantive challenge.
mapped_messages: Example asked; Example?; A concrete example is requested.

definition: A concrete example is provided.
boolean_question: Does this move provide a concrete example for an abstract claim?
yes_meaning: The move names a specific case (city, year, person, document) that exemplifies an abstract claim, in service of supporting the claim.
no_meaning: The move provides general evidence (provides_evidence), or asks for an example, or makes the abstract claim alone.
mapped_messages: Example provided; Example +; A concrete example is provided.

definition: This move makes a claim about evidence.
boolean_question: Does this move make a substantive claim ABOUT evidence — its existence, quality, applicability, or interpretation?
yes_meaning: The move's claim is about evidence itself: "the evidence shows X"; "no evidence supports Y"; "the data is mixed". Meta-evidence framing.
no_meaning: The move uses evidence to support a substantive non-evidence claim, asks for evidence, or makes a claim without evidence reference.
mapped_messages: Evidence claim; Evid claim; This move makes a claim about evidence.

definition: This move makes a claim that would normally require evidence but does not provide one.
boolean_question: Does this move make a factual / empirical / statistical claim WITHOUT providing a source, quote, study citation, or other evidence?
yes_meaning: The move asserts a fact, a statistic, a causal claim, or a specific historical event without attaching evidence and without explicitly marking it as opinion / hypothesis / hedged.
no_meaning: (a) source / quote / link attached, OR (b) explicitly hedged ("I think", "probably"), OR (c) value claim, OR (d) pure question.
mapped_messages: Evidence gap; Evid gap; This move makes a claim that would normally require evidence but does not provide one.

definition: This move repairs a prior source-chain gap by adding the primary source.
boolean_question: Does this move repair a prior source-chain gap by adding the primary source or filling the missing link?
yes_meaning: A prior move had creates_source_chain_gap. This move provides the primary source or fills the missing chain link.
no_meaning: The move provides general evidence without addressing a prior gap, opens new debt, or makes a non-evidence claim.
mapped_messages: Source chain repaired; Chain fix; This move repairs a prior source-chain gap by adding the primary source.

definition: This move uses an anecdote as support.
boolean_question: Does this move use a personal anecdote or single-case story as support for a claim?
yes_meaning: The move provides a single experiential case ("when I worked at X", "I knew someone who", "in my city") in support of a broader claim.
no_meaning: The move provides systematic evidence, multiple cases, or makes a claim without an anecdote.
mapped_messages: Anecdote; This move uses an anecdote as support.

definition: This move uses a statistic as support.
boolean_question: Does this move use a statistic (percentage, ratio, rate, count, average) as support for a claim?
yes_meaning: The move cites a numerical statistic in support of a claim.
no_meaning: The move makes a claim without statistics, OR mentions a number that is not statistical (a year, a phone number, an ID).
mapped_messages: Statistic; This move uses a statistic as support.

definition: This move appeals to an external authority as evidence.
boolean_question: Does this move appeal to an external authority (named expert, institution, body of work) as evidence for a claim?
yes_meaning: The move cites an external authority (the IPCC; the World Bank; a named expert) in support of a claim. Distinct from authority_reasoning_present (Family E scheme) — this is the evidence-act, not the scheme.
no_meaning: The move provides primary data without authority appeal, OR uses authority reasoning without an external authority citation.
mapped_messages: External authority; Ext auth; This move appeals to an external authority as evidence.

definition: This move questions the quality of evidence the parent cited.
boolean_question: Does this move challenge the QUALITY of evidence the parent cited (methodology, sample size, source credibility)?
yes_meaning: The move targets the evidence itself: "that study has methodological problems"; "the sample size was small"; "the source is partisan". Distinct from disputes_evidence_applicability (which accepts quality but disputes fit).
no_meaning: The move accepts the evidence quality and disputes applicability (disputes_evidence_applicability), or makes a substantive counter-claim, or accepts both quality and applicability.
mapped_messages: Evidence quality questioned; Qual?; This move questions the quality of evidence the parent cited.

definition: This move invokes a burden-of-proof argument.
boolean_question: Does this move invoke a burden-of-proof argument — claiming the parent has the burden to prove a position and has not met it?
yes_meaning: "The burden of proof is on you"; "until you show X, the default is Y"; "extraordinary claims require extraordinary evidence". Meta-evidence framing about who must produce evidence.
no_meaning: The move asks for evidence directly (asks_for_evidence), provides evidence, or makes a substantive challenge.
mapped_messages: Burden of proof; Burden?; This move invokes a burden-of-proof argument.

definition: This move uses causal reasoning as its primary support.
boolean_question: Does this move use cause-and-effect reasoning (X causes Y) as its primary support?
yes_meaning: The move argues from a cause to its effect, or from an effect to a likely cause. The causal claim is doing the inferential work, not merely incidental.
no_meaning: The move uses correlation without claiming causation, evidence without inferential structure, or definitional / analogical / value reasoning.
mapped_messages: Causal reasoning; Causal; This move uses causal reasoning as its primary support.

definition: This move uses an analogy as its primary support.
boolean_question: Does this move use an analogy (mapping the case under discussion to a different case) as its primary form of support?
yes_meaning: The move advances its position by drawing a comparison to a different case / domain / situation, with the implication that what is true of the comparison applies to the case at hand.
no_meaning: The move uses direct evidence, examples-of-the-same-case, definitional argument, or causal reasoning. A passing comparison ('like a library') without inferential weight is NOT analogy reasoning.
mapped_messages: Analogy reasoning; Analogy; This move uses an analogy as its primary support.

definition: This move uses one or more examples as its primary support.
boolean_question: Does this move use one or more concrete examples-of-the-same-kind as its primary support?
yes_meaning: The move advances a generalization or claim and supports it with specific examples from the same domain (not analogical mappings to a different domain).
no_meaning: The move uses analogical mapping (analogy_reasoning_present), direct numerical evidence, or definitional / causal reasoning. A passing example without inferential weight is not example reasoning.
mapped_messages: Example reasoning; Example; This move uses one or more examples as its primary support.

definition: This move appeals to an authority as its primary support.
boolean_question: Does this move appeal to an authority (expert, institution, body of literature) as its primary support, where the inferential weight rests on the authority?
yes_meaning: The move cites an authority and the argument rests on the authority's credibility: 'the IPCC says X'; 'leading economists agree'; 'the medical consensus is'. The authority is the warrant, not just an attribution.
no_meaning: The move cites an authority along with substantive evidence the authority used (mixed authority + evidence), or paraphrases an authority's reasoning without leaning on the authority's status.
mapped_messages: Authority reasoning; Authority; This move appeals to an authority as its primary support.

definition: This move argues from the consequences of an action.
boolean_question: Does this move argue from the predicted consequences of an action or policy as its primary support?
yes_meaning: The move advocates for or against an option based on predicted consequences: 'X will lead to Y, and Y is good/bad'. The consequence prediction is doing the inferential work.
no_meaning: The move appeals to principle, definition, or precedent rather than consequence; or notes consequences as side facts without basing the argument on them.
mapped_messages: Consequence reasoning; Consequence; This move argues from the consequences of an action.

definition: This move argues from a principle or rule.
boolean_question: Does this move argue from a stated principle or general rule, applying the principle to the case at hand?
yes_meaning: The move identifies a principle ('all people should be treated equally'; 'free speech requires content-neutral platforms') and argues the case follows from the principle.
no_meaning: The move argues from consequences, evidence, or examples rather than a principle. Mention of principle as background without it being the warrant is not principle reasoning.
mapped_messages: Principle reasoning; Principle; This move argues from a principle or rule.

definition: This move argues from a definition.
boolean_question: Does this move argue from a definition (the case at hand falls under or out of a definition, and the conclusion follows)?
yes_meaning: The move applies a definition: 'X falls under the definition of Y, therefore X has Y\'s properties / treatments'. The definitional fit is the warrant.
no_meaning: The move uses evidence, principle, consequence, or example rather than definition. Mention of a definition as background is not definition reasoning.
mapped_messages: Definition reasoning; Definition; This move argues from a definition.

definition: This move argues from a classification.
boolean_question: Does this move argue by classifying the case into a category and applying category-level conclusions?
yes_meaning: The move classifies the case ('this is a federal jurisdiction matter'; 'this is a tort case, not a contract case') and applies the category's general treatment.
no_meaning: The move applies a definition without classification (definition_reasoning_present), or uses evidence within an accepted category, or reasons by case-specific facts without categorization.
mapped_messages: Classification reasoning; Classify; This move argues from a classification.

definition: This move argues from precedent.
boolean_question: Does this move argue from precedent (a prior decision or established practice that should govern the present case)?
yes_meaning: The move identifies a precedent and argues the present case should follow it: 'in case X, the court decided Y; the present case is similar, so Y should apply'. The precedent is the warrant.
no_meaning: The move uses a precedent as background or comparison without claiming it should govern. The move argues from first principles or fresh evidence.
mapped_messages: Precedent reasoning; Precedent; This move argues from precedent.

definition: This move argues by means-end (practical) reasoning.
boolean_question: Does this move use means-end (practical) reasoning — arguing that a specific action will achieve a stated end?
yes_meaning: The move identifies a goal and argues a specific action is the means to achieve it: 'we want X; doing Y will achieve X'. Walton's practical reasoning scheme.
no_meaning: The move argues from consequences as good/bad (consequence_reasoning_present) rather than goal-instrument fit, or uses other schemes.
mapped_messages: Means-end reasoning; Means-end; This move argues by means-end (practical) reasoning.

definition: This move uses tradeoff reasoning.
boolean_question: Does this move argue by explicitly balancing tradeoffs between competing values, costs, or outcomes?
yes_meaning: The move identifies multiple competing considerations and argues for a position based on the balance: 'X gives us A but costs us B; on balance A outweighs B because...'.
no_meaning: The move advances one consideration without balancing, dismisses the competing one, or uses cost-benefit specifically (cost_benefit_reasoning_present).
mapped_messages: Tradeoff reasoning; Tradeoff; This move uses tradeoff reasoning.

definition: This move uses abductive (inference-to-best-explanation) reasoning.
boolean_question: Does this move use abductive reasoning — inferring the best explanation for observed evidence?
yes_meaning: The move identifies an observation and infers a likely cause / explanation as the best account: 'we see X; the best explanation for X is Y'. Inference to best explanation.
no_meaning: The move uses direct causal claim without explanatory framing (causal_reasoning_present), or uses observation as evidence for a hypothesized cause without claiming it is the BEST explanation.
mapped_messages: Abductive explanation; Abductive; This move uses abductive (inference-to-best-explanation) reasoning.

definition: This move argues by identifying an exception to a general rule.
boolean_question: Does this move argue that the present case is an exception to a general rule the parent invoked?
yes_meaning: The parent invoked a general rule; the move argues the present case is an exception: 'usually X, but here the special circumstance Y makes X not apply'. Toulmin rebuttal scheme.
no_meaning: The move challenges the general rule itself (challenges_parent), or extends the rule, or proposes a different rule.
mapped_messages: Exception reasoning; Exception; This move argues by identifying an exception to a general rule.

definition: This move argues via a chain of consequences (slippery slope).
boolean_question: Does this move argue against a proposed action by claiming it will lead to a chain of further consequences, each leading to the next?
yes_meaning: The move argues 'if we do X, that will lead to X1, which will lead to X2, which will lead to a bad final state'. A multi-step consequence chain.
no_meaning: The move argues from a single direct consequence (consequence_reasoning_present) or from independent multiple consequences not linked in a chain.
mapped_messages: Slippery-slope reasoning; Slippery; This move argues via a chain of consequences (slippery slope).

definition: This move uses cost-benefit reasoning.
boolean_question: Does this move use explicit cost-benefit reasoning — quantifying costs and benefits and arguing the net is positive or negative?
yes_meaning: The move identifies measurable costs and measurable benefits and argues for or against based on the net. Common in policy and economic reasoning.
no_meaning: The move uses qualitative tradeoff (tradeoff_reasoning_present) without quantifying, or argues from principle / consequence without cost-benefit framing.
mapped_messages: Cost-benefit reasoning; Cost-benefit; This move uses cost-benefit reasoning.

definition: This move uses risk-based reasoning.
boolean_question: Does this move argue by identifying a risk (probability × magnitude of harm) and using the risk as primary support?
yes_meaning: The move identifies a low-probability high-magnitude outcome (or vice versa) and argues from the risk: 'X has a 5% chance of catastrophic outcome Y; that risk justifies precaution'.
no_meaning: The move argues from direct consequence prediction (consequence_reasoning_present) or expected value (cost_benefit_reasoning_present) without framing the argument around risk.
mapped_messages: Risk reasoning; Risk; This move uses risk-based reasoning.

definition: The reasoning link between this move's grounds and its claim is not explicit.
boolean_question: Does this move present a claim + grounds without making the warrant (the rule licensing claim from grounds) explicit?
yes_meaning: The move provides grounds (data / evidence / examples) and a claim, but the reasoning link between them is implicit. A reader could reasonably ask 'WHY does that ground support that claim?'.
no_meaning: (a) Grounds + explicit warrant present, OR (b) pure question / clarification / paraphrase, OR (c) claim trivially entailed by ground, OR (d) pure value claim without empirical ground.
mapped_messages: Warrant not explicit; Warrant?; The reasoning link between this move's grounds and its claim is not explicit.

definition: This move relies on an assumption it does not state.
boolean_question: Does this move's reasoning depend on an assumption that the move does not explicitly state?
yes_meaning: The move's reasoning requires a premise to hold, but the premise is not stated in the move. Identifying the assumption opens productive inquiry.
no_meaning: The move states all premises explicitly, the move is a pure question / paraphrase, or the move uses a premise that is universally shared in the context.
mapped_messages: Unstated assumption; Assump?; This move relies on an assumption it does not state.

definition: This move appeals to an authority without grounding the appeal.
boolean_question: Does this move appeal to an authority without naming the authority's specific expertise or basis for the claim at hand?
yes_meaning: The move cites 'experts say' / 'scientists agree' / 'leading economists' without naming who or what backs the appeal. Walton's critical question for the authority scheme.
no_meaning: The move names the specific authority and the relevant expertise, OR does not use authority reasoning at all, OR cites the authority alongside the specific evidence the authority used.
mapped_messages: Authority basis missing; Auth?; This move appeals to an authority without grounding the appeal.

definition: This move claims causation without proposing a mechanism.
boolean_question: Does this move claim cause-and-effect without proposing a mechanism that explains how the cause produces the effect?
yes_meaning: The move asserts X causes Y but does not propose HOW: through what process, what intermediate steps. Walton critical question for causal scheme.
no_meaning: The move proposes a mechanism (X causes Y through the process P), OR notes correlation without claiming causation, OR uses non-causal reasoning.
mapped_messages: Causal mechanism missing; Mech?; This move claims causation without proposing a mechanism.

definition: This move uses an analogy without specifying the mapping.
boolean_question: Does this move use an analogy without specifying WHICH features of the source case map to the target case?
yes_meaning: The move draws an analogy (X is like Y) but does not specify what features of Y bear on X. Walton critical question for analogy scheme.
no_meaning: The move specifies the mapping (X is like Y in respect R, which is relevant because...), OR does not use analogy at all.
mapped_messages: Analogy mapping missing; Map?; This move uses an analogy without specifying the mapping.

definition: This move uses examples without showing they are representative.
boolean_question: Does this move use examples to support a generalization without showing the examples are representative of the relevant population?
yes_meaning: The move cites examples but does not address whether the examples generalize: 'Pittsburgh and Detroit show X' (do they?). Walton critical question for example scheme.
no_meaning: The move addresses representativeness (cites that the examples are typical, or notes selection criteria), OR uses single-case reasoning, OR does not use example reasoning.
mapped_messages: Example representativeness unclear; Rep?; This move uses examples without showing they are representative.

definition: This move predicts consequences without addressing their probability.
boolean_question: Does this move predict consequences without addressing the probability that those consequences will occur?
yes_meaning: The move predicts an outcome ('X will lead to Y') without giving any sense of how likely Y is. Walton critical question for consequence + slippery-slope schemes.
no_meaning: The move addresses probability (cites studies, gives base rates, hedges with modal language), OR is descriptive rather than predictive.
mapped_messages: Consequence probability unclear; Prob?; This move predicts consequences without addressing their probability.

definition: This move applies a definition without showing the case fits within boundaries.
boolean_question: Does this move apply a definition to a case without showing the case clearly falls within the definition's boundaries?
yes_meaning: The move applies a definition but the boundary case is debatable: 'libraries are infrastructure' — is this case clearly within the definition? Walton critical question for definition scheme.
no_meaning: The move addresses the boundary explicitly (cites why the case fits), OR uses a clear case, OR does not use definition reasoning.
mapped_messages: Definition boundary unclear; Bound?; This move applies a definition without showing the case fits within boundaries.

definition: This move applies multiple criteria without showing how they are weighted.
boolean_question: Does this move apply multiple decision criteria without showing how they are weighted against each other?
yes_meaning: The move invokes more than one criterion (cost AND effectiveness; equity AND efficiency) but does not say how to weight them when they conflict.
no_meaning: The move addresses the weighting (provides a weighting rule), OR uses a single criterion, OR does not invoke decision criteria.
mapped_messages: Criterion weighting unclear; Weight?; This move applies multiple criteria without showing how they are weighted.

definition: This move infers a cause without addressing alternative explanations.
boolean_question: Does this move infer a cause from observed effects without addressing plausible alternative explanations?
yes_meaning: The move infers cause from effect (abductive reasoning) but does not address alternatives that could account for the same effect. Standard critical question for abductive reasoning.
no_meaning: The move addresses alternatives (rules them out, weighs them), OR does not use abductive reasoning, OR uses controlled comparison that addresses confounds.
mapped_messages: Alternative explanation available; Alt expl?; This move infers a cause without addressing alternative explanations.

definition: This move advances a generalization where counterexamples are known.
boolean_question: Does this move advance a generalization where there are known counterexamples not addressed in the move?
yes_meaning: The move makes a generalization (X is universally true, X always happens) but a counterexample exists in the public record. Standard critical question for generalization claims.
no_meaning: The move addresses known counterexamples (rules them out, explains them as edge cases), OR avoids generalization, OR no known counterexamples exist.
mapped_messages: Counterexample available; CE?; This move advances a generalization where counterexamples are known.

definition: This move makes a claim without stating its scope limits.
boolean_question: Does this move make a claim without stating the population, time window, or setting limits within which the claim holds?
yes_meaning: The move states a claim without scope qualifiers: 'X is true' (everywhere? always? for all people?). Pairs with Family H claim_specificity_low.
no_meaning: The move scopes the claim explicitly (in cities of >100K; over the 2015-2020 window; for non-emergency contexts), OR makes a universal claim that is genuinely intended as universal.
mapped_messages: Scope limit unstated; Scope?; This move makes a claim without stating its scope limits.

definition: This move makes an unqualified claim where qualification would be appropriate.
boolean_question: Does this move state a claim without modal qualification ("usually", "often", "in most cases") in a context where the underlying evidence supports only qualified claims?
yes_meaning: The move asserts a claim categorically (X is Y) but the evidence base supports only qualified versions (X is usually Y; X is Y in most cases). Pairs with Family H modal_language_present (positive structural counterpart).
no_meaning: The move uses modal language appropriately, OR the underlying claim is genuinely categorical (definitional, mathematical).
mapped_messages: Qualification missing; Qual?; This move makes an unqualified claim where qualification would be appropriate.

definition: This move uses a comparison without specifying the baseline.
boolean_question: Does this move use a comparison (better, more, larger, lower) without specifying the baseline of comparison?
yes_meaning: The move says 'X is better' / 'X is larger' / 'X is more effective' without naming what X is being compared to.
no_meaning: The move specifies the baseline (X is more effective than Y; X is larger than the 2019 baseline), OR uses absolute claims that do not require comparison.
mapped_messages: Comparison baseline missing; Base?; This move uses a comparison without specifying the baseline.

definition: A branch is suggested for this exchange.
boolean_question: Has the system suggested branching from this point?
yes_meaning: Auto-metadata: heuristic detected that the cluster would benefit from branching (multiple axes active).
no_meaning: No branch suggestion recorded.
mapped_messages: Branch hint; Branch suggested; A branch is suggested for this exchange.

yes_meaning: Auto-metadata: a participant took the branching action; a new sub-tree begins here.
no_meaning: No branching action taken at this point.

yes_meaning: Auto-metadata: no posts in the cluster for N hours / days (per system threshold).
no_meaning: Recent activity in the cluster.

no_meaning: Cluster has new content / angles per turn.

no_meaning: No confirmation, or confirmation only from same side (not cross-side).

yes_meaning: Lifecycle: cluster has common ground + remaining narrow differences; synthesis may resolve productively.
no_meaning: No synthesis-ready signal.

no_meaning: New content per turn.

definition: This cluster is archived or resolved.
